problems.name: Systemic lupus erythematosus, evolving (UCTD with progression)
problems.diagnosedBy: Dr. Jessica Tanaka (Rheumatology, 4/10/2026); evolving phenotype per Dr. Andre Williams (PCP, 9/15/2026) and ED 11/4/2026
problems.notes: Initial presentation 1/12/2026 with symmetric inflammatory polyarthritis, alopecia, low-titer ANA. Rheumatology working dx 4/10/2026 was undifferentiated CTD / probable early SLE. Phenotype evolving: by 9/15/2026 had mild cytopenias and low albumin; by 11/4/2026 with new-onset anti-dsDNA positive (24), low complements (C3 76, C4 8), mild proteinuria (UPC 0.35), tri-lineage cytopenia, photosensitive rash. Now meeting more 2019 EULAR/ACR classification criteria for SLE. Highly impacted by access barriers and medication non-adherence cycles.

problems.name: Alopecia, diffuse non-scarring
problems.notes: Diffuse non-scarring scalp hair thinning. Concurrent with arthritis symptoms. Has progressed since onset.

problems.name: Hashimoto's thyroiditis (autoimmune thyroiditis)
problems.diagnosedBy: Confirmed at urgent care follow-up (Dr. Reyes) based on TPO antibody positivity (312, ref <34)
problems.notes: TSH 5.32 at initial presentation. TPO antibody markedly positive at 312 (ref <34) on send-out lab 1/26/2026. Patient on levothyroxine 25 mcg daily. Adherence intermittent; TSH oscillates 3.18 (4/10) -> 4.21 (9/15) -> 5.04 (11/4).

problems.name: Loss of health insurance coverage
problems.notes: Patient lost Medicaid coverage during pandemic redetermination. Medicaid re-application denied 5/29/2026; appeal hearing 8/12/2026. Major barrier to medication continuity and specialty care. Significantly impacts disease management.

problems.name: Medication non-adherence due to access barriers
problems.notes: Hydroxychloroquine refill could not be completed 8/22/2026 due to PAP renewal denial. Out of HCQ from 8/12/2026 to 9/15/2026, then again from approximately 10/15/2026 to 11/4/2026. Restart via ED-pharmacy charity bridge supply 11/4/2026.

problems.name: Unintentional weight loss
problems.diagnosedBy: First documented at rheumatology consult (Dr. Tanaka); progressed substantially
problems.notes: Trajectory: 50.8 kg (1/12) -> 48.8 kg (4/10) -> 46.4 kg (9/15) -> 43.8 kg (11/4). Total loss 7.0 kg / 13.8% over 10 months. Contributing factors: inflammatory disease, food insecurity, decreased appetite, stress. Concerning for systemic illness in addition to caloric insufficiency.

problems.name: Depression, moderate (provisional)
problems.notes: PHQ-9 11 at 9/15/2026 PCP visit (moderate). PHQ-9 14 at 11/4/2026 ED triage (moderately severe). Declined antidepressant pharmacotherapy; accepted Spanish-speaking therapist referral. No SI/HI.

reviewedBy: Dr. Priya Shah (ED, 11/4/2026) — updated to reflect evolving SLE phenotype, medication non-adherence, weight loss, and depression.
